summary: The notebook demonstrates a complete, production-ready multi-agent architecture for an intelligent chat system using Google's Gemini API. It structures the workflow into distinct, modular components: intent classification, dynamic routing, knowledge retrieval, and session memory. The author emphasizes robust error handling and deterministic LLM calls to ensure system stability against API interruptions and policy violations. Readers should learn how to orchestrate specialized agents, constrain LLM outputs via strict system prompts, and implement a resilient message routing pipeline.
competition_flow: Initializes a Google Gemini client, constructs a modular multi-agent pipeline for intent classification and response routing, and validates the end-to-end message handling through a CLI wrapper and test cases.
insights: Constraining LLM outputs to specific intent tags using temperature=0.0 and strict system prompts yields deterministic routing behavior.
Decoupling agents into specialized modules (moderator, knowledge retriever, router) simplifies debugging and improves system resilience.
Implementing explicit safety and API error checks in the moderator layer prevents cascading failures and ensures graceful degradation.
